content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To     :  File                                    Date  :  Sept. 30, 1983

                                                  No. :  M-83-223


From  :  FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
         Barbara A. Milman

Subject:  Advice to Vance Raye 9/29/83


      Steve Merksamer and several other high-level Deukmejian
aides are going to the Republican Convention.  They will be given
air transportation by a private individual, accommodations by the
Republican Party.  They will not be making speeches so it is not
a situation in which they are receiving honoraria.

      Because the Deukmejian Campaign Committee could send them to
the convention (and would if it were the only way to avoid
reporting the air fare as a personal gift to each aide) we
advised that the following reporting would satisfy the Act:

      1.  The air transportation and accommodations should be
reported as in-kind contributions to the Deukmejian committee.

      2.  If the value of the air transportation and
accommodations are $250 or more for any one aide, that aide must
report that he has received reimbursement for expenses from the
Deukmejian Campaign Committee on the income schedule of his next
Statement of Economic Interests.

      3.  The value of private air transportation is the amount
that the transportation would cost on a commercial air line.  See
Stone Opinion, 3 FPPC Opinions 52, No. 77-003.

BAM:plh

William R. Attwater
December 6, 1977                                                Page Two


     Metropolitan Water District is a "local government
agency" as defined in §82041.

     Initially, we observe that §82030(b)(2) excludes
reimbursement for expenses from the definition of income
even if the reimbursement is provided by a public agency
other than the official's employer. Thus, if the tours
are "reimbursement for expenses", they will not constitute
reportable income to the members of the Regional Water
Quality Control Board despite the fact that they were pro-
vided by Metropolitan Water District.

     However, the tours could be considered to be "gifts"
not "reimbursement for expenses". If the tours con-
stitute "gifts", they must be reported as income pursuant
to the 82030(a) definition of income. We believe that the
tours in the instant case are best characterized as reim-
bursement for expenses. We note that the tours in question
are of water related facilities and that the officials are
performing official duties while on such tours. Moreover,
we think it is significant that the parties involved are
both public agencies concerned with the administration of
water facilities in California. Accordingly, we conclude
that the tours are reimbursements for expenses from a
state or local government agency and, pursuant to the
exemption contained in §82030(b)(2), need not be reported.

     If I can be of further assistance in this or any other
case, please contact me.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Kenneth W. Goshorn
                                        Research Specialist III
                                        Conflicts of Interest Division


KWG:glb

sections.conclusion: The air transportation and accommodations should be reported as in-kind contributions to the Deukmejian committee. If the value of air transportation and accommodations are $250 or more for any aide, that aide must report reimbursement for expenses from the Deukmejian Campaign Committee on the income schedule of the next Statement of Economic Interests. The value of private air transportation is the amount that the transportation would cost on a commercial airline.
sections.question_synthetic: What reporting requirements apply when high-level government aides receive air transportation and accommodations from a private individual and the Republican Party to attend the Republican convention?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: FPPC advised that the air transportation and accommodations should be reported as in-kind contributions to the Deukmejian committee, with individual aides reporting reimbursement for expenses if the value exceeds $250, valued at commercial airline rates.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document appears to be internal FPPC memorandum dated 9/30/83 documenting advice given to Vance Raye on 9/29/83. Contains clear reporting guidance but lacks formal question/conclusion sections typical of advice letters. Second page appears to be from a different document (William R. Attwater, December 6, 1977) regarding water district tours and was likely included in error during scanning/transmission.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What reporting requirements apply when high-level government aides receive air transportation and accommodations from a private individual and the Republican Party to attend the Republican convention?

CONCLUSION: The air transportation and accommodations should be reported as in-kind contributions to the Deukmejian committee. If the value of air transportation and accommodations are $250 or more for any aide, that aide must report reimbursement for expenses from the Deukmejian Campaign Committee on the income schedule of the next Statement of Economic Interests. The value of private air transportation is the amount that the transportation would cost on a commercial airline.

Milman Subject: Advice to Vance Raye 9/29/83 Steve Merksamer and several other high-level Deukmejian aides are going to the Republican Convention. They will be given air transportation by a private individual, accommodations by the Republican Party. They will not be making speeches so it is not a situation in which they are receiving honoraria. Because the Deukmejian Campaign Committee could send them to the convention (and would if it were the only way to avoid reporting the air fare as a personal gift to each aide) we advised that the following reporting would satisfy the Act: 1. The air transportation and accommodations should be reported as in-kind contributions to the Deukmejian committee. 2. If the value of the air transportation and accommodations are $250 or more for any one aide, that aide must report that he has received reimbursement for expenses from the Deukmejian Campaign Committee on the income schedule of his next Statement of Economic Interests. 3. Attwater December 6, 1977 Page Two Metropolitan Water District is a "local government agency" as defined in §82041. Initially, we observe that §82030(b)(2) excludes reimbursement for expenses from the definition of income even if the reimbursement is provided by a public agency other than the official's employer. Thus, if the tours are "reimbursement for expenses", they will not constitute reportable income to the members of the Regional Water Quality Control Board despite the fact that they were pro- vided by Metropolitan Water District. However, the tours could be considered to be "gifts" not "reimbursement for expenses". If the tours con- stitute "gifts", they must be reported as income pursuant to the 82030(a) definition of income. We believe that the tours in the instant case are best characterized as reim- bursement for expenses. We note that the tours in question are of water related facilities and that the officials are performing official duties while on such tours. Moreover, we think it is significant that the parties involved are both public agencies concerned with the administration of water facilities in California. Accordingly, we conclude that the tours are reimbursements for expenses from a state or local government agency and, pursuant to the exemption contained in §82030(b)(2), need not be reported. If I can be of further assistance in this or any other case, please contact me. Sincerely, [signature] Kenneth W. Goshorn Research Specialist III Conflicts of Interest Division KWG:glb
embedding.summary: FPPC memorandum providing advice on reporting requirements for air transportation and accommodations provided to Deukmejian aides attending the Republican convention, advising that these should be reported as in-kind contributions with specific thresholds for individual reporting.
